Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.html

Annotation Interface Html

@Documented @Target({TYPE,FIELD,METHOD}) @Retention(RUNTIME) @Inherited public @interface Html
Annotation that can be applied to classes, fields, and methods to tweak how they are handled by HtmlSerializer.

Can be used in the following locations:

  • Marshalled classes/methods/fields.
  • @Rest-annotated classes and @RestOp-annotated methods when used with @HtmlApply.
See Also:
  • @Html Annotation
  • HTML Basics

  • Optional Element Summary

    Optional Elements
    Modifier and Type
    Optional Element
    Description
    String
    anchorText
    Use the specified anchor text when serializing a URI.
    String[]
    description
    Optional description for the exposed API.
    HtmlFormat
    format
    Specifies what format to use for the HTML element.
    String
    link
    Adds a hyperlink to a bean property when rendered as HTML.
    boolean
    noTableHeaders
    When true, don't add headers to tables.
    boolean
    noTables
    When true, collections of beans should be rendered as trees instead of tables.
    Class<? extends HtmlRender>
    render
    Associates an HtmlRender with a bean property for custom HTML rendering of the property.
    String
    style
    Specifies the CSS style to apply to the HTML element containing the bean property value.

  • Element Details

    • anchorText

      String anchorText
      Use the specified anchor text when serializing a URI.

      The text can contain any bean property values resolved through variables of the form "{property-name}".

      Example:

      // Produces <a href='...'>drive</a> when serialized to HTML. @Html(anchorText="drive") @URI // Treat property as a URL public String getDrive() {...}

      This overrides the behavior specified by HtmlSerializer.Builder.uriAnchorText(AnchorText).

      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • description

      String[] description
      Optional description for the exposed API.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Since:
      9.2.0
      Default:
      {}

    • format

      HtmlFormat format
      Specifies what format to use for the HTML element.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      HTML

    • link

      String link
      Adds a hyperlink to a bean property when rendered as HTML.

      The text can contain any bean property values resolved through variables of the form "{property-name}".

      The URLs can be any of the following forms:

      • Absolute - e.g. "http://host:123/myContext/myServlet/myPath"
      • Context-root-relative - e.g. "/myContext/myServlet/myPath"
      • Context-relative - e.g. "context:/myServlet/myPath"
      • Servlet-relative - e.g. "servlet:/myPath"
      • Path-info-relative - e.g. "myPath"
      Example:

      public class FileSpace { @Html(link="servlet:/drive/{drive}") public String getDrive() { ...; } }

      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • noTableHeaders

      boolean noTableHeaders
      When true, don't add headers to tables.
      See Also:
      • HtmlSerializer.Builder.addKeyValueTableHeaders()
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      false

    • noTables

      boolean noTables
      When true, collections of beans should be rendered as trees instead of tables.

      Default is false.

      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      false

    • render

      Class<? extends HtmlRender> render
      Associates an HtmlRender with a bean property for custom HTML rendering of the property.

      This annotation applies to bean properties and classes.

      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      org.apache.juneau.marshall.html.HtmlRender.class

    • style

      String style
      Specifies the CSS style to apply to the HTML element containing the bean property value.

      This is a simpler alternative to using render() when you only need to apply CSS styling without custom content transformation.

      Example:

      // Apply styling to a bean property @Html(style="white-space:normal;min-width:200px") public String getField() {...}

      If both style() and render() are specified, the render takes precedence.

      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""
